chapter_name: A Tale of Three Intersecting Lines
intro_text: Hi there! I'm Rishika, and today we explore triangles — the strongest shape in nature! 🔺 From the pyramids of Egypt to bridges and rooftops, triangles are everywhere. Let's discover their amazing properties!
complete_message: Outstanding! You've mastered A Tale of Three Intersecting Lines! 🏆

explain_questions.question: What is a triangle? What are its basic elements?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What is a triangle? What are its basic elements?

steps.t: A triangle is a closed figure with 3 sides, 3 angles and 3 vertices.
steps.s: A triangle is a closed figure formed by three line segments. It has 3 sides, 3 angles, and 3 vertices.

steps.t: Named by vertices: triangle ABC has vertices A, B, C; sides AB, BC, CA.
steps.s: A triangle is named by its vertices. Triangle ABC has vertices A, B and C, and sides AB, BC and CA.

steps.t: The sum of all three angles of a triangle is always 180 degrees.
steps.s: The most important property: the sum of all three interior angles of any triangle is always 180 degrees.

explain_questions.confirm_question: How many sides does a triangle have?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: How many sides does a triangle have?
nudges: Think about what TRI means.
TRI means three in Latin.
A triangle has 3 sides, 3 angles and 3 vertices!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q1s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Triangle: 3 sides, 3 angles, 3 vertices</text></g><g id="q1s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Triangle ABC: sides AB, BC, CA</text></g><g id="q1s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Sum of all angles = 180° (always!)</text></g><g id="q1ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">3 sides</text></g>

explain_questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 60 degrees and angle B = 80 degrees. Find angle C.
explain_questions.question_spoken: In triangle ABC, angle A equals 60 degrees and angle B equals 80 degrees. Find angle C.

steps.t: Angle sum property: angle A + angle B + angle C = 180 degrees.
steps.s: By the angle sum property of triangles, all three angles add up to 180 degrees.

steps.t: 60 + 80 + angle C = 180. So 140 + angle C = 180.
steps.s: Substituting: 60 plus 80 plus angle C equals 180. So 140 plus angle C equals 180.

steps.t: Angle C = 180 - 140 = 40 degrees.
steps.s: Angle C equals 180 minus 140 which is 40 degrees.

explain_questions.confirm_question: In a triangle, two angles are 55 and 75 degrees. Find the third angle.
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: In a triangle, two angles are 55 and 75 degrees. Find the third angle.
nudges: All three angles add up to 180 degrees.
55 + 75 = 130. So the third angle = 180 - 130.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q2s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Angle sum: A + B + C = 180°</text></g><g id="q2s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">60 + 80 + C = 180 → C = 40°</text></g><g id="q2s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Angle C = 40 degrees</text></g><g id="q2ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">40 degrees</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What are the types of triangles based on angles?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What are the types of triangles based on angles?

steps.t: Acute triangle: ALL three angles are less than 90 degrees.
steps.s: An acute triangle has all three angles less than 90 degrees. Every angle is sharp.

steps.t: Right triangle: ONE angle is exactly 90 degrees.
steps.s: A right triangle has exactly one angle that is 90 degrees. The side opposite the right angle is the hypotenuse.

steps.t: Obtuse triangle: ONE angle is greater than 90 degrees.
steps.s: An obtuse triangle has one angle greater than 90 degrees. The other two angles must both be acute.

explain_questions.confirm_question: A triangle has angles 30, 60 and 90 degrees. What type is it?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: A triangle has angles 30, 60 and 90 degrees. What type is it?
nudges: Look at the largest angle in the triangle.
One of the angles is exactly 90 degrees.
A triangle with one 90-degree angle is a right triangle!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q3s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Acute: all angles < 90°</text></g><g id="q3s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Right: one angle = 90°</text></g><g id="q3s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Obtuse: one angle > 90°</text></g><g id="q3ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">Right triangle</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What are the types of triangles based on sides?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What are the types of triangles based on sides?

steps.t: Equilateral triangle: ALL three sides are equal. All angles = 60 degrees.
steps.s: An equilateral triangle has all three sides equal. Since all sides are equal, all three angles are also equal, each measuring 60 degrees.

steps.t: Isosceles triangle: ANY two sides are equal. Base angles are equal.
steps.s: An isosceles triangle has two equal sides. The angles opposite the equal sides are also equal, called base angles.

steps.t: Scalene triangle: ALL three sides are different. All angles are different.
steps.s: A scalene triangle has all three sides of different lengths. All three angles are also different.

explain_questions.confirm_question: A triangle has two equal sides of 5 cm and a third side of 7 cm. What type is it?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: A triangle has two equal sides of 5 centimetres and a third side of 7 centimetres. What type is it?
nudges: Count how many sides are equal.
Two sides are equal (5 cm each) and one is different (7 cm).
A triangle with two equal sides is isosceles!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q4s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Equilateral: all 3 sides equal, all angles 60°</text></g><g id="q4s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Isosceles: 2 sides equal, base angles equal</text></g><g id="q4s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Scalene: all sides different, all angles different</text></g><g id="q4ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">Isosceles triangle</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What is the exterior angle of a triangle? State the exterior angle theorem.
explain_questions.question_spoken: What is the exterior angle of a triangle? State the exterior angle theorem.

steps.t: Exterior angle: formed by one side and the extension of an adjacent side.
steps.s: An exterior angle of a triangle is formed by one side of the triangle and the extension of an adjacent side.

steps.t: Exterior Angle Theorem: exterior angle = sum of two non-adjacent interior angles.
steps.s: The Exterior Angle Theorem states that the exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two interior angles that are not adjacent to it.

steps.t: Example: If angles A=50 and B=70, exterior angle at C = 50+70 = 120 degrees.
steps.s: For example, if angle A is 50 degrees and angle B is 70 degrees, the exterior angle at C equals 50 plus 70 which is 120 degrees.

explain_questions.confirm_question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 45 and angle B = 65. Find the exterior angle at C.
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: In triangle ABC, angle A equals 45 degrees and angle B equals 65 degrees. Find the exterior angle at C.
nudges: Exterior angle equals sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles.
The non-adjacent angles to C are A and B.
45 + 65 = 110 degrees!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q5s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Exterior angle: outside the triangle</text></g><g id="q5s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Exterior angle = sum of 2 non-adjacent interior angles</text></g><g id="q5s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">50 + 70 = 120° (exterior at C)</text></g><g id="q5ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">110 degrees</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What is the median of a triangle?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What is the median of a triangle?

steps.t: Median: a line from a vertex to the MIDPOINT of the opposite side.
steps.s: A median of a triangle is a line segment drawn from a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

steps.t: Every triangle has 3 medians, one from each vertex.
steps.s: Since a triangle has 3 vertices, it has 3 medians, one from each vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

steps.t: All 3 medians meet at one point called the CENTROID (centre of gravity).
steps.s: All three medians intersect at a single point called the centroid, which is the centre of gravity of the triangle.

explain_questions.confirm_question: How many medians does a triangle have?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: How many medians does a triangle have?
nudges: A median goes from each vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.
How many vertices does a triangle have?
3 vertices means 3 medians!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q6s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Median: vertex to midpoint of opposite side</text></g><g id="q6s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">3 medians in every triangle</text></g><g id="q6s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">All 3 meet at CENTROID</text></g><g id="q6ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">3 medians</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What is the altitude of a triangle?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What is the altitude of a triangle?

steps.t: Altitude: perpendicular from a vertex to the opposite side (or its extension).
steps.s: The altitude of a triangle is the perpendicular line segment drawn from a vertex to the opposite side or to the extension of the opposite side.

steps.t: Every triangle has 3 altitudes, one from each vertex.
steps.s: A triangle has 3 altitudes, one from each vertex, each perpendicular to the opposite side.

steps.t: All 3 altitudes meet at a point called the ORTHOCENTRE.
steps.s: All three altitudes meet at a single point called the orthocentre.

explain_questions.confirm_question: What is the name of the point where all three altitudes of a triangle meet?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: What is the name of the point where all three altitudes of a triangle meet?
nudges: All three altitudes meet at a special point.
It starts with the letter O.
The point is called the orthocentre!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q7s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Altitude: perpendicular from vertex to opposite side</text></g><g id="q7s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">3 altitudes in every triangle</text></g><g id="q7s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">All 3 meet at ORTHOCENTRE</text></g><g id="q7ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">Orthocentre</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What is the triangle inequality? Can a triangle have sides 3, 4 and 8?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What is the triangle inequality? Can a triangle have sides 3, 4 and 8 centimetres?

steps.t: Triangle inequality: sum of any two sides must be GREATER than the third side.
steps.s: The triangle inequality states that the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the third side.

steps.t: Check: 3+4=7. Is 7 > 8? No! 7 is less than 8.
steps.s: For sides 3, 4 and 8: check 3 plus 4 equals 7. Is 7 greater than 8? No, 7 is less than 8.

steps.t: Since 3+4 is NOT greater than 8, these sides CANNOT form a triangle.
steps.s: Since the sum of two sides 3 and 4 is not greater than the third side 8, these measurements cannot form a triangle.

explain_questions.confirm_question: Can a triangle have sides 5, 7 and 11?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Can a triangle have sides 5, 7 and 11 centimetres?
nudges: Check if sum of any two sides is greater than the third.
Check the smallest two: 5 + 7 = 12. Is 12 > 11?
12 > 11. Yes, a triangle is possible!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q8s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Triangle inequality: sum of 2 sides > third side</text></g><g id="q8s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">3+4=7, is 7 > 8? No!</text></g><g id="q8s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Cannot form a triangle!</text></g><g id="q8ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">No, cannot form triangle</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What are the properties of an equilateral triangle?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What are the properties of an equilateral triangle?

steps.t: All 3 sides are equal. All 3 angles are equal = 60 degrees each.
steps.s: An equilateral triangle has all three sides equal and all three angles equal. Each angle is exactly 60 degrees.

steps.t: It is also isosceles (any two sides are equal).
steps.s: An equilateral triangle is a special case of isosceles triangle because any two of its sides are equal.

steps.t: Its median, altitude, perpendicular bisector from each vertex all coincide.
steps.s: In an equilateral triangle, the median, altitude and perpendicular bisector from each vertex all fall on the same line.

explain_questions.confirm_question: Each angle of an equilateral triangle is how many degrees?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Each angle of an equilateral triangle is how many degrees?
nudges: All three angles are equal and they add up to 180 degrees.
180 divided by 3 = ?
180 / 3 = 60 degrees each!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q9s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Equilateral: all sides equal, all angles = 60°</text></g><g id="q9s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Also qualifies as isosceles</text></g><g id="q9s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Median = altitude = perpendicular bisector</text></g><g id="q9ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">60 degrees</text></g>

explain_questions.question: The angles of a triangle are in ratio 1:2:3. Find all three angles.
explain_questions.question_spoken: The angles of a triangle are in ratio 1:2:3. Find all three angles.

steps.t: Let angles be x, 2x and 3x. Sum = 180 degrees.
steps.s: Let the angles be x, 2x and 3x. By angle sum property, they must add to 180 degrees.
x plus 2x plus 3x equals 6x equals 180. So x equals 30 degrees.

steps.t: Angles are 30, 60 and 90 degrees. It is a right-angled triangle!
steps.s: The three angles are 30, 60 and 90 degrees. Since one angle is 90 degrees, it is a right-angled triangle!

explain_questions.confirm_question: Angles of a triangle are in ratio 2:3:4. Find the largest angle.
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Angles of a triangle are in ratio 2:3:4. Find the largest angle.
nudges: Let angles be 2k, 3k and 4k. Their sum is 180.
Largest angle = 4k = 4 x 20 = 80 degrees!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q10s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Ratio 1:2:3 → angles x, 2x, 3x</text></g><g id="q10s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">6x = 180 → x = 30</text></g><g id="q10s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Angles: 30, 60, 90 ° (right triangle!)</text></g><g id="q10ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">30, 60 and 90 degrees</text></g>

practice_questions.question: Two angles of a triangle are 47 and 63 degrees. Find the third angle.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Two angles of a triangle are 47 and 63 degrees. Find the third angle.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Add the two known angles and subtract from 180. Simple as that!

practice_questions.question: Classify the triangle with angles 35, 55 and 90 degrees.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Classify the triangle with angles 35, 55 and 90 degrees.
steps: A triangle with one 90-degree angle is a right triangle.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Check the largest angle only! Less than 90 = acute, equals 90 = right, more than 90 = obtuse.
practice_questions.alt: One angle is exactly 90 degrees, so it is a right-angled triangle.

practice_questions.question: In triangle PQR, the exterior angle at Q is 115 degrees and angle P = 55 degrees. Find angle R.
practice_questions.question_spoken: In triangle PQR, the exterior angle at Q is 115 degrees and angle P equals 55 degrees. Find angle R.
steps: Exterior angle at Q = angle P + angle R (exterior angle theorem).
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Exterior angle = sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. Rearrange to find the unknown!
practice_questions.alt: Also: interior angle at Q = 180 - 115 = 65. Then angle R = 180 - 55 - 65 = 60 degrees.

practice_questions.question: Can a triangle have sides 6 cm, 8 cm and 15 cm?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Can a triangle have sides 6 centimetres, 8 centimetres and 15 centimetres?
steps: Check: sum of two smallest sides = 6 + 8 = 14.
Triangle inequality fails, so these sides cannot form a triangle.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Only check the two smallest sides! Their sum must be greater than the largest side.
practice_questions.alt: 6 + 8 = 14. Since 14 < 15, the triangle inequality is violated. No triangle possible.

practice_questions.question: An isosceles triangle has base angles of 50 degrees each. Find the vertex angle.
practice_questions.question_spoken: An isosceles triangle has base angles of 50 degrees each. Find the vertex angle.
steps: Base angles of isosceles triangle are equal: both 50 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: In isosceles triangle, base angles are equal. Vertex angle = 180 - 2 x (base angle)!

practice_questions.question: Each angle of an equilateral triangle is how many degrees?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Each angle of an equilateral triangle is how many degrees?
steps: Equilateral triangle has all three angles equal.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Equilateral means equal. Three equal angles summing to 180. So each = 60!
practice_questions.alt: All angles equal and sum to 180. So each angle = 60 degrees.

practice_questions.question: The exterior angle of a triangle is 130 degrees. If one non-adjacent interior angle is 70 degrees, find the other.
practice_questions.question_spoken: The exterior angle of a triangle is 130 degrees. If one non-adjacent interior angle is 70 degrees, find the other non-adjacent interior angle.
steps: Exterior angle = sum of two non-adjacent interior angles.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Exterior angle = sum of remote interior angles. Subtract one to find the other!

practice_questions.question: The angles of a triangle are in ratio 3:4:5. Find all three angles.
practice_questions.question_spoken: The angles of a triangle are in ratio 3:4:5. Find all three angles.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Add ratio parts (3+4+5=12). Divide 180 by 12 to get k. Then multiply each part by k!

practice_questions.question: In a right-angled triangle, one acute angle is 35 degrees. Find the other acute angle.
practice_questions.question_spoken: In a right-angled triangle, one acute angle is 35 degrees. Find the other acute angle.
steps: Right triangle has one angle = 90 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are always complementary (add to 90)! So 90 - 35 = 55.
practice_questions.alt: The two acute angles add to 90. So 90 - 35 = 55 degrees.

practice_questions.question: Triangle ABC has angle A = 2x, angle B = 3x and angle C = 4x. Find x and all angles.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Triangle ABC has angle A equal to 2x, angle B equal to 3x and angle C equal to 4x. Find x and all three angles.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Add the coefficients (2+3+4=9). Divide 180 by 9 to find x directly!

practice_questions.question: Identify the type: a triangle with sides 5 cm, 5 cm and 5 cm.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Identify the type: a triangle with sides 5 centimetres, 5 centimetres and 5 centimetres.
steps: All three sides are equal (5 cm each).
A triangle with all sides equal is equilateral.
All angles are also equal at 60 degrees each.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Equal sides = equilateral. If all three sides same, all three angles are also same (60 each)!
practice_questions.alt: Three equal sides means equilateral triangle with all angles 60 degrees.

practice_questions.question: The centroid divides each median in ratio 2:1 from vertex. If a median is 9 cm, find the distance from vertex to centroid.
practice_questions.question_spoken: The centroid divides each median in ratio 2:1 from the vertex. If a median is 9 centimetres long, find the distance from vertex to centroid.
steps: Centroid divides median in ratio 2:1 from vertex.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Centroid is always 2/3 of the way from vertex to midpoint. So vertex to centroid = 2/3 of median!
practice_questions.alt: 2/3 x 9 = 6 cm from vertex to centroid.

practice_questions.question: In triangle ABC, AB = AC. If angle A = 40 degrees, find angle B.
practice_questions.question_spoken: In triangle ABC, AB equals AC. If angle A equals 40 degrees, find angle B.
steps: AB = AC means triangle is isosceles. Angles B and C are equal.
Angle A + angle B + angle C = 180.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Equal sides opposite equal angles. AB=AC means angle B = angle C. Use angle sum to find them!

practice_questions.question: Can a triangle be both right-angled and equilateral?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Can a triangle be both right-angled and equilateral?
steps: Equilateral triangle has all angles = 60 degrees.
Right triangle must have one angle = 90 degrees.
60 is not 90, so a triangle cannot be both equilateral and right-angled.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Equilateral forces all angles to 60. Right-angled forces one to 90. These two conditions contradict each other!
practice_questions.alt: Equilateral = all angles 60. Right-angled = one angle 90. Impossible to have both.

practice_questions.question: In triangle XYZ, exterior angle at Z = 3x + 10 and the two non-adjacent interior angles are x + 20 and x + 30. Find x.
practice_questions.question_spoken: In triangle XYZ, the exterior angle at Z equals 3x plus 10 and the two non-adjacent interior angles are x plus 20 and x plus 30. Find x.
steps: Exterior angle = sum of non-adjacent interior angles.
3x - 2x = 50 - 10, so x = 40. Check: exterior = 130, interior sum = 60 + 70 = 130.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Set exterior angle equal to sum of the two given interior angles and solve for x!
practice_questions.alt: 3x+10 = 2x+50, x = 40. Verify: exterior = 130, interior sum = 60+70 = 130. Correct!
